It also supports Ollama and user-deployed models. - Allows setting different AI models for different tools; for example, local models can be used for translation, while DeepSeek can be used for in-depth research. - Allows control and authorization of project folders accessed by AI, enhancing data privacy and security. - Allows editing of AI translation and rewriting results; AI can remember personal style and [...] modern, minimalist, and stylish feel - AI-Assisted Writing: translation, proofreading, rewriting, and creative writing - AI Deep Research: analysis of PDF content, and generation of reference citations. - Visual Writing: Write intuitively with Project Outline, Card Library, Graph, and Timeline - Non-linear: Allows for chapter-based or card-based writing, with flexible organization and adjustment structure at any time - Rich Editor: Supports text style, inline image and formula, footnote, comment, highlighting, multi-column, and text formatting - High Performance: A single article can reach 100,000 words without lag - Block Elements: Use the "/" syntax to insert heading, text, list, image, code block, table, math formula, bibliography etc - Style Template: Precisely set styles, font, font [...] Lattics is designed for professionals in highly educated such as writers, art critics, bloggers, university students, scientific researchers, engineers, financial analysts etc, significantly improving the efficiency of writing novels, academic papers, research reports, project reports, and knowledge learning Lattics' design principles follow the divergent thinking and the structured info organization of the human brain, providing an immersive, multi-perspective, multi-dimensional, and interconnected info to stimulate creative efficiency. results.title: What Is Lattics?
results.content:
Traditional tools make this worse. Files are isolated. Folders impose rigid hierarchies. Notes are either too structured (databases) or too loose (plain text). And when it's time to turn your research into a paper or report, you're starting from scratch — copying, pasting, and reorganizing everything manually.

There had to be a better way.

## Why "Brain-like"? A Design Philosophy, Not Just a Feature

Lattics isn't built around file systems or database schemas. It's built around a simple insight: your brain doesn't store knowledge in folders. It stores knowledge as a network of interconnected neurons, where each idea is linked to related ideas, and information is retrieved by association, not by navigating a directory tree. [...] Lattics

Blog

Pricing

# What Is Lattics?

A knowledge management and writing app designed around how your brain naturally thinks, connects, and creates.

## The Problem with How We Work Today

If you're a researcher, writer, or knowledge worker, you've likely felt this: your ideas are scattered across files, folders, apps, and tabs. To connect one insight to another, you have to switch contexts — opening a new file, searching through folders, or jumping between browser tabs. Every context switch flushes your working memory and breaks your flow. [...] Decades later, that vision remains largely unrealized. Most tools still organize knowledge like filing cabinets — not like brains. Lattics is our attempt to finally build the knowledge management system that Bush and Engelbart imagined: one that works with your mind, not against it.

## Start Thinking in Lattics

Free to start — no account required.

Free download for Windows

Also available for macOS

Or learn more on the pricing page.

Lattics - Brain-like Academic and Creative Writing

@ 2026Aura Marker Studio

results.title: Lattics | Its Function “Sucks”, But The Brain Helps
results.content:
Although they provided a Block, it’s a Block with chaotic logic and incomplete functionality. It appears that the Lattics team hasn’t used popular block-based note-taking software like Notion, Heptabase, Capacities, or Feishu. We are also unsure whether there is anyone in the team who writes frequently.

There are also some minor issues. For example, when we change a line to a title by using “#”, we cannot revert it to plain text by pressing “Backspace” at the beginning of the line, nor can we select the title to transform it back to text. Instead, we can only click the six dots at the beginning of the line and then “convert” it to text. [...] Faced with these three views, I’m not quite sure if I’m operating correctly. If you’ve previously used Obsidian’s Canvas, Heptabase, or Scrintal, you might find Lattics confusing. You won’t clearly know which type of view you “should” be using, or if you are “correctly” using the current view.

## It Helps

Although Lattics may not be an excellent note-taking software, it is actually quite good for writing.

Lattics is particularly suitable for “traditional” people — those who have difficulty understanding new things or are reluctant to change their long-established writing habits using local software like Word.

Lattics still retains the retro love for magazines. In its document styles, it offers three magazine-style options: The Economist, Brooklyn, and Medium. [...] ### Finally

Finally, I hope Lattics can continue to survive in the current “note-taking arena.” It should understand its strengths and user base rather than spreading its efforts too thin.

Of course, don’t forget the mobile platform. Look, even Heptabase is now focusing on mobile. Mobile should be a battleground for note-taking software with card-like structures. With a mobile app, we can record our thoughts anytime, anywhere, and effortlessly. If voice functionality and voice-to-text are added in the future, that would be even better! Who needs Notability then? (Certainly not me, haha).

Nowadays, almost all note-taking software is incorporating AI functionalities, while local writing software like iA Writer and Ulysses don’t seem to have such plans.
